name: Greater Sydney
industryNote: Diverse metropolitan economy spanning financial services, professional services, retail, IT, construction and light manufacturing. Sydney CBD hosts most ASX-listed company headquarters in NSW.

name: Inner West
industryNote: Mixed-use urban precincts with strong professional services, creative industries, hospitality and increasingly tech.

name: Western Sydney
industryNote: Logistics and light manufacturing heartland of NSW. Major freight terminals, intermodal hubs and food production facilities serving Greater Sydney and beyond.

name: North Shore
industryNote: Concentration of professional services, IT and corporate offices, particularly around Chatswood and Macquarie Park.

name: Eastern Suburbs
industryNote: Professional services, hospitality, retail and tourism. Significant inbound logistics through Port Botany.

name: Northern Beaches
industryNote: Professional services, retail, hospitality and small-scale manufacturing.

name: South Western Sydney
industryNote: Major logistics, transport and manufacturing corridor anchored by Liverpool, Bankstown and Fairfield. Significant food processing.

name: Sutherland Shire
industryNote: Mixed economy with concentration of small business, retail and trade services. Major hospital and aged care presence.

name: Hunter Region
industryNote: Coal mining heartland and major power generation centre. Significant wine industry (Hunter Valley), agriculture, port logistics (Newcastle), aluminium and shipbuilding.

name: Illawarra
industryNote: Steelmaking (BlueScope at Port Kembla), coal mining, port logistics, manufacturing and growing university sector. Major Scope 3 supply chain pressures from BlueScope.

name: Central Coast
industryNote: Mixed economy including manufacturing, retail, hospitality and growing healthcare. Commuter region for Sydney workforce.

name: Central West
industryNote: Diverse agriculture (sheep, cattle, grain, wine, horticulture), gold mining (Orange-Bathurst), agricultural services and food processing.

name: New England
industryNote: Pastoral country with fine wool, beef cattle, sheep and grain. Education centres (University of New England in Armidale) and a growing renewable energy zone.

name: Northern Rivers
industryNote: Sugarcane, dairy, beef, macadamias and horticulture. Strong tourism and creative industries presence around Byron Bay and Lismore.

name: St George
industryNote: Residential southern Sydney with retail, hospitality, trades and small business services.

name: Mid North Coast
industryNote: Tourism, retirement and healthcare services, dairy, oyster aquaculture, forestry and a growing aged-care sector.

name: Murray
industryNote: Irrigated agriculture along the Murray River — rice, citrus, wine grapes, dairy and stone fruit. Significant food processing capacity.

name: Riverina
industryNote: Major irrigated agriculture region — rice (SunRice), wine (Riverina is Australia's largest wine-producing region by volume), cotton, beef, sheep and grain. Critical for retail and processor Scope 3 supply chains.

name: Far West
industryNote: Mining (Broken Hill silver-lead-zinc), pastoral grazing and tourism. Sparse population, long supply chains.

name: Lower North Shore
industryNote: Concentration of professional services, corporate offices, hospitality and small business. Significant healthcare presence.

name: Northern Sydney
industryNote: Professional services, IT and corporate offices, particularly the Macquarie Park business park. Major university and healthcare presence.

name: Upper North Shore
industryNote: Predominantly residential with strong professional services, education and healthcare.

name: Hills District
industryNote: Growing commercial and residential precincts. Major retail, professional services and emerging logistics presence.

name: Macarthur
industryNote: Growth corridor for housing, logistics and light manufacturing in south-west Sydney.

name: Capital Region
industryNote: Surrounds and supplies the ACT — government services, agriculture, defence-related industry and growing renewable energy projects (Snowy Mountains).

name: Greater Melbourne
industryNote: Australia's largest manufacturing centre, with strong food processing, advanced manufacturing, financial services, professional services, education and healthcare.

name: Inner North
industryNote: Creative industries, hospitality, professional services and growing tech sector.

name: Inner West
industryNote: Historic industrial port precincts transitioning to mixed-use. Logistics, light manufacturing and emerging creative and hospitality industries.

name: Bayside
industryNote: Predominantly residential with retail, hospitality and professional services.

name: Barwon
industryNote: Geelong manufacturing heartland transitioning post-automotive — now wind energy components, advanced manufacturing, port logistics (Geelong Port), agriculture and tourism.

name: Central Highlands
industryNote: Historic gold mining (Ballarat). Mixed agriculture, education, healthcare and manufacturing.

name: Loddon
industryNote: Agriculture (grains, livestock), manufacturing (Bendigo), gold mining heritage and growing renewable energy projects.

name: Mornington Peninsula
industryNote: Wine (cool-climate Pinot Noir and Chardonnay), tourism, hospitality and horticulture.

name: Inner South
industryNote: Mixed residential and commercial with professional services, retail and hospitality.

name: Inner East
industryNote: Professional services, education, healthcare and retail.

name: South East
industryNote: Growth corridor with logistics, manufacturing and retail.

name: Western Melbourne
industryNote: Major freight and logistics corridor, light manufacturing and significant population growth.

name: Mallee
industryNote: Broadacre cropping (wheat, barley), almonds (Australia's largest production area near Mildura), table grapes, citrus and dryland and irrigated horticulture.

name: Inner South-East
industryNote: Residential and commercial with retail, hospitality and professional services.

name: Inner North-West
industryNote: Mixed residential, retail and emerging professional services.

name: Eastern Melbourne
industryNote: Suburban commercial precincts with strong professional services, retail and emerging tech and healthcare.

name: South East Melbourne
industryNote: Major growth corridor with logistics, manufacturing, retail and emerging healthcare.

name: North-Western Melbourne
industryNote: Outer growth corridor with retail, logistics and population-driven services.

name: Goulburn Valley
industryNote: Critical Australian food bowl — pome and stone fruit, dairy processing (SPC, Murray Goulburn), tomatoes and vegetable production. Concentrated processing infrastructure around Shepparton.

name: North East
industryNote: Cool-climate wine (King Valley, Beechworth, Rutherglen), beef cattle, sheep, tobacco heritage industries transitioning, and tourism.

name: Western District
industryNote: Sheep grazing (fine wool), dairy, beef cattle, cropping and forestry. Port logistics around Portland and Warrnambool.

name: Gippsland
industryNote: Dairy heartland (Murray Goulburn, Saputo), brown coal power generation (Latrobe Valley), gas, forestry, beef and horticulture.

name: Greater Brisbane
industryNote: South-east Queensland economic engine — financial services, professional services, retail, healthcare, education and a growing tech sector.

name: South East Queensland
industryNote: Population growth corridor including the Gold Coast and Sunshine Coast — tourism, construction, retail, healthcare and a growing tech sector.

name: Far North Queensland
industryNote: Sugarcane (Mossman, Mareeba), tropical horticulture (bananas, mangoes), tourism (Cairns, Port Douglas), bauxite (Weipa) and fisheries.

name: North Queensland
industryNote: Sugarcane, cattle, mining services, defence (Townsville), and growing renewable energy projects.

name: Darling Downs
industryNote: Major broadacre cropping (wheat, sorghum, cotton), feedlot beef, intensive horticulture and pork. Concentrated food processing and grain handling around Toowoomba.

name: Central Queensland
industryNote: Bowen Basin coal mining services, beef cattle, agriculture, and the Gladstone industrial precinct (alumina refining, LNG).

name: Wide Bay Burnett
industryNote: Sugarcane (Bundaberg), horticulture (macadamias, citrus, vegetables), aquaculture, tourism (Hervey Bay, Fraser Coast) and forestry.

name: Gold Coast
industryNote: Tourism, hospitality, construction, retail and emerging marine industry. Significant film and creative sectors.

name: Inner Brisbane
industryNote: Brisbane CBD-adjacent professional services, hospitality, retail and corporate offices.

name: Moreton Bay
industryNote: Growth corridor with retail, light manufacturing, healthcare and residential construction.

name: Redland City
industryNote: Residential bayside region with retail, hospitality and growing healthcare.

name: Logan City
industryNote: Growth corridor with logistics, light manufacturing, retail and residential construction.

name: Sunshine Coast
industryNote: Tourism, hospitality, construction, healthcare and emerging creative and tech sectors.

name: Gladstone Region
industryNote: Major industrial precinct — alumina refining (Rio Tinto Yarwun, QAL), LNG export terminals (Curtis Island), cement, port logistics. One of Australia's most energy-intensive industrial clusters.

name: Central Highlands
industryNote: Coal mining services (Bowen Basin), cattle grazing, agriculture and resources logistics.

name: Capricorn Coast
industryNote: Tourism, beef cattle, fisheries and supporting industry for Central Queensland mining and Gladstone industry.

name: North West Queensland
industryNote: Mount Isa lead-zinc-copper-silver mining and processing, beef cattle, and mining services.

name: Isaac Region
industryNote: Heart of the Bowen Basin coal mining industry — major employer for fly-in fly-out workforce. Supplier-intensive economy serving BHP, Glencore, Anglo American operations.

name: Whitsunday Region
industryNote: Tourism (Great Barrier Reef), sugarcane (Proserpine, Bowen), tropical horticulture and port logistics (Hay Point coal terminal at Mackay edge).

name: Burdekin Region
industryNote: Sugarcane (largest cane region in Australia by tonnage), beef cattle and irrigated horticulture.

name: Hinchinbrook Region
industryNote: Sugarcane, beef cattle and tropical horticulture.

name: Cassowary Coast
industryNote: Tropical horticulture (bananas, pawpaw, exotic fruits), sugarcane, fisheries and tourism.

name: Tablelands
industryNote: Cool-climate horticulture (vegetables, dairy, coffee, avocados), beef cattle and an emerging high-value food sector.

name: Cook Shire
industryNote: Cattle grazing, fisheries, mining (silica sand) and tourism in remote north Queensland.

name: Torres Strait
industryNote: Fisheries (prawn, lobster, mackerel), government services and Indigenous economic enterprises.

name: Maranoa
industryNote: Coal seam gas (Roma), beef cattle, sheep, grains and supporting services for energy industry.

name: Murweh Shire
industryNote: Beef cattle, sheep, mineral exploration and pastoral country.

name: Central West Queensland
industryNote: Beef cattle (Longreach is the historic centre of the Australian cattle industry), sheep and mineral exploration.

name: Gulf Country
industryNote: Beef cattle, prawn fisheries (Karumba), zinc and base metal mining, and pastoral country.

name: Greater Perth
industryNote: Western Australia's economic centre — mining services and head offices (BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside), construction, financial services and growing tech.

name: Northern Perth
industryNote: Growing residential corridor with retail, healthcare and emerging logistics.

name: Peel
industryNote: Alumina refining (Alcoa Pinjarra), agriculture transitioning to industrial, mineral sands, and tourism.

name: South West
industryNote: Wine (Margaret River — premium Bordeaux varietals), dairy, forestry, mining (mineral sands), tourism and education.

name: Mid West
industryNote: Iron ore mining (Geraldton port export), pastoral grazing, wheat, fishing (rock lobster) and emerging renewable energy projects.

name: Goldfields-Esperance
industryNote: Gold mining (Kalgoorlie's Super Pit and surrounds — world's most productive gold region), nickel, lithium and port logistics (Esperance).

name: Kimberley
industryNote: Pastoral cattle, pearling, tourism, oil and gas (Browse Basin), iron ore exploration and Indigenous-led tourism enterprises.

name: Coastal Perth
industryNote: Tourism, hospitality, retail and residential. Education and emerging marine industry.

name: Inner Perth
industryNote: Perth CBD-adjacent professional services, hospitality, retail and corporate offices.

name: Southern Perth
industryNote: Mixed residential, retail and emerging logistics and light manufacturing.

name: South Eastern Perth
industryNote: Industrial and growth corridor with logistics, light manufacturing and population services.

name: Eastern Perth
industryNote: Industrial precincts, transport and logistics, retail and residential growth.

name: Western Suburbs
industryNote: Predominantly residential with retail, professional services and hospitality.

name: Great Southern
industryNote: Wine (Mount Barker, Frankland River), wool, broadacre grains and emerging aquaculture.

name: Pilbara
industryNote: Iron ore mining heartland — Rio Tinto, BHP, Fortescue and Roy Hill major operations. Port Hedland and Dampier are the world's largest iron ore export ports. Supplier-intensive economy with high Scope 3 expectations.

name: Gascoyne
industryNote: Pastoral cattle, horticulture (Carnarvon banana and table grape production), fisheries (rock lobster, prawns) and tourism.

name: Greater Adelaide
industryNote: Defence manufacturing (Osborne naval shipbuilding), advanced manufacturing, wine, food processing, healthcare and growing renewable energy.

name: Western Adelaide
industryNote: Defence manufacturing, port logistics (Port Adelaide), advanced manufacturing and growing renewable hydrogen industry.

name: Limestone Coast
industryNote: Wine (Coonawarra Cabernet Sauvignon), forestry and timber processing (Mount Gambier), dairy and beef cattle.

name: Eyre Peninsula
industryNote: Broadacre grains (one of Australia's most productive wheat regions), tuna fishing and aquaculture (Port Lincoln), pastoral grazing and mining.

name: Far North
industryNote: Mining (Olympic Dam copper-uranium near Roxby Downs), pastoral cattle, sheep and gas exploration.

name: Fleurieu Peninsula
industryNote: Wine (McLaren Vale Shiraz and Grenache), tourism, horticulture and dairy.

name: Eastern Adelaide
industryNote: Predominantly residential with professional services, retail and hospitality.

name: Barossa
industryNote: World-renowned wine region — Shiraz and old-vine Grenache. Concentrated grape-growing, winemaking and ancillary industries. Strong export market exposure to EU and Asia.

name: Clare Valley
industryNote: Wine (Riesling, Shiraz, Cabernet Sauvignon) and cool-climate grape growing. Tourism, olives and broadacre agriculture.

name: Mid North
industryNote: Broadacre cropping (wheat, barley, canola), sheep and mining (Burra historic copper).

name: Greater Hobart
industryNote: Government services, tourism, education (UTAS), antarctic and marine research, food processing and growing creative sector.

name: Northern Tasmania
industryNote: Dairy (in the Tamar Valley and around Launceston), beef, wine (Tamar Valley sparkling and Pinot Noir), aquaculture (Atlantic salmon) and timber.

name: North West
industryNote: Dairy (Murray Goulburn, Fonterra), vegetables (Devonport and Burnie are major potato and onion growing areas), aquaculture, zinc smelting (Hobart and Bell Bay), and forestry.

name: Top End
industryNote: LNG (Bayu-Undan, Ichthys), aluminium (Gove), pastoral cattle, fisheries (prawn, barramundi), tourism, port logistics (Darwin) and significant defence presence.

name: Central Australia
industryNote: Pastoral cattle, tourism (Uluru), Indigenous-led enterprises and defence (Pine Gap).

name: Katherine Region
industryNote: Pastoral cattle, irrigated horticulture (mangoes, melons), defence (RAAF Tindal) and tourism.

name: East Arnhem
industryNote: Bauxite mining (Rio Tinto Gove), Indigenous-led tourism and enterprises, and fisheries.

name: Capital Region
industryNote: Federal government services, defence, professional services, education and growing tech sector. The ACT operates on 100% renewable electricity.

name: North Canberra
industryNote: Federal government precincts, education (ANU), and growing professional services and hospitality.

name: South Canberra
industryNote: Federal government precincts, defence and growing residential and retail.
